LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EDWIN GEE'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF DEFENDANT EDWIN M. GEES MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT UNDER C.C.P. 473.5 IS DENIED. "A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION MUST BE BASED ON NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW, AND FACTS OF WHICH THE PARTY SEEKING RECONSIDERATION WAS AWARE AT THE TIME OF THE ORIGINAL RULING ARE NOT NEW OR DIFFERENT." (IN RE MARRIAGE OF HERR (2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 1463, 1468.) ADDITIONALLY, "A PARTY MUST PROVIDE A SATISFACTORY EXPLANATION FOR FAILING TO OFFER THE EVIDENCE IN THE FIRST INSTANCE." (ID.) DEFENDANT DOES NOT SET FORTH NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS THAT WERE NOT AVAILABLE TO HIM AT THE TIME OF THE INITIAL HEARING. FURTHER, DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE A SATISFACTORY EXPLANATION AS TO WHY THIS EVIDENCE WAS NOT PROVIDED IN THE FIRST INSTANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendant Edwin M. Gees Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Default Under C.C.P. 473.5
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Blair K. Walsh, of Abramson Smith Waldsmith LLP, Tel. 415.421.7995, for plaintiffs;
Thoms W.J. Purtell, of The Law Offices of Thomas W. J. Purtell, Tel. 415.722.6291, for defendants.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT EDWIN GEE'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendant Edwin M. Gees Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Default Under C.C.P. 473.5 is denied. "A motion for reconsideration must be based on new or different facts, circumstances, or law, and facts of which the party seeking reconsideration was aware at the time of the original ruling are not new or different." (In re Marriage of Herr (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1463, 1468.) Additionally, "a party must provide a satisfactory explanation for failing to offer the evidence in the first instance." (Id.) Defendant does not set forth new or different facts that were not available to him at the time of the initial hearing. Further, Defendant does not provide a satisfactory explanation as to why this evidence was not provided in the first instance. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
